title: 1.3 Major Discoveries that Revolutionized Biology
description: Comprehensive coverage of twelve major discoveries that revolutionized biology, including Aristotle, Galen, Leeuwenhoek, Linnaeus, Darwin, Mendel, Pasteur, Koch, Goodall, McClintock, Watson and Crick, and Wilmut and Campbell.

questions.question: What was the classification system developed by Aristotle known as until the nineteenth century?
What did Aristotle establish for the first time in biology?
options: Species relationships and grouped them correctly
questions.correctAnswer: Species relationships and grouped them correctly

questions.question: Aristotle classified organisms based on which criteria?
options: Their ability to move from one place to another and their characteristics as herbs, shrubs, and trees
questions.correctAnswer: Their ability to move from one place to another and their characteristics as herbs, shrubs, and trees

questions.question: What time period did Aristotle live in?
Galen's efforts transformed which field of science?
Which medical specialties did Galen significantly impact?
Among Galen's important findings were the distinctions between which two types of blood vessels?
Galen recognized that which organ produces voice?
What time period did Galen live in?
What is Antonie van Leeuwenhoek well renowned for?
options: His contributions to microscopy and applying it to biology
questions.correctAnswer: His contributions to microscopy and applying it to biology

questions.question: According to some sources, how much could Leeuwenhoek's strong lenses magnify?
Among Leeuwenhoek's findings using microscopes were which discoveries?
options: Bacteria and the vacuole of the cell
questions.correctAnswer: Bacteria and the vacuole of the cell

questions.question: What time period did Antonie van Leeuwenhoek live in?
What professions did Carl Linnaeus hold?
What system did Carl Linnaeus devise?
options: The method for naming, ordering, and classifying creatures
questions.correctAnswer: The method for naming, ordering, and classifying creatures

questions.question: Linnaeus separated items into which three categories?
How did Linnaeus organize living things?
options: Into classes, orders, genera, and species
questions.correctAnswer: Into classes, orders, genera, and species

questions.question: In the name Homo sapiens, which part is the genus?
What time period did Carl Linnaeus live in?
What did Charles Darwin establish about all species of life?
options: They were created by God at the beginning of the world
They descended over time from common ancestors
They remain completely unchanged over time
They are all unrelated to each other
questions.correctAnswer: They descended over time from common ancestors

questions.question: According to Darwin, species continue to exist through which process?
In what year was Darwin's theory of evolution published in On the Origin of Species?
What long-held belief was Darwin disputing with his theory?
options: That all species had been created by God at the beginning of the world
That species could change over time
That fossils were remains of ancient organisms
questions.correctAnswer: That all species had been created by God at the beginning of the world

questions.question: What is the combination of evolution by natural selection with Mendelian genetics called?
What time period did Charles Darwin live in?
What organism did Gregor Mendel use to discover and demonstrate the laws of genetic inheritance?
What terms did Gregor Mendel coin in the process of discovering the laws of inheritance?
When were Mendel's laws rediscovered?
options: At the turn of the 19th century
At the turn of the 20th century
questions.correctAnswer: At the turn of the 20th century

questions.question: What time period did Gregor Mendel live in?
What is Louis Pasteur regarded as?
What did Louis Pasteur pioneer research on?
What did Louis Pasteur demonstrate about bacteria?
options: That they are all harmful to humans
That they cause fermentation and disease
That they cannot survive in the presence of air
That they are classified as plants
questions.correctAnswer: That they cause fermentation and disease

questions.question: What process did Louis Pasteur introduce?
Which industries did Louis Pasteur rescue in France?
Which vaccines did Louis Pasteur develop?
What time period did Louis Pasteur live in?
Robert Koch was a well-known German physician who pioneered which field?
What is Robert Koch credited as?
Which diseases did Robert Koch pinpoint the precise causal agents of?
What did Robert Koch provide experimental support for?
What time period did Robert Koch live in?
Jane Goodall is a UK specialist in which field?
What did Jane Goodall discover about chimpanzees?
options: That they are herbivores and cannot use tools
That they are omnivores and tool users
questions.correctAnswer: That they are omnivores and tool users

questions.question: What notable academic achievement did Jane Goodall accomplish?
options: She was the first woman to win the Nobel Prize
She was awarded a PhD from the University of Cambridge without holding a bachelor's degree
She discovered the structure of DNA
She founded the field of genetics
questions.correctAnswer: She was awarded a PhD from the University of Cambridge without holding a bachelor's degree

questions.question: In what year was Jane Goodall born?
What type of scientist was Barbara McClintock?
What plant did Barbara McClintock spend her career analyzing?
What technique did Barbara McClintock develop?
options: A technique for identifying and examining chromosomes individually
questions.correctAnswer: A technique for identifying and examining chromosomes individually

questions.question: What did Barbara McClintock's work make possible?
options: The cloning of the first mammal

questions.question: What award did Barbara McClintock receive in 1983?
options: The Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine

questions.question: Barbara McClintock is known for her discovery of which phenomenon?
options: Transposition and how genes could turn their physical characteristics on and off
The double helix structure of DNA
questions.correctAnswer: Transposition and how genes could turn their physical characteristics on and off

questions.question: What time period did Barbara McClintock live in?
What discovery made James Watson and Francis Crick famous in 1962?
options: The discovery of the structure of DNA
The discovery of the circulation of blood
questions.correctAnswer: The discovery of the structure of DNA

questions.question: What prize did Watson and Crick win for their discovery?
Watson and Crick's model of DNA describes it as having what shape?
What does the double helix model of DNA explain?
options: How DNA replicates and hereditary information is coded and passed on to descendants
questions.correctAnswer: How DNA replicates and hereditary information is coded and passed on to descendants

questions.question: The discovery of DNA structure has been used in which areas?
options: Disease diagnosis and treatment, forensics, and more
questions.correctAnswer: Disease diagnosis and treatment, forensics, and more

questions.question: What time period did Francis Crick live in?
Which mammal did Ian Wilmut and Keith Campbell clone in 1996?
How did Wilmut and Campbell clone Dolly?
options: Using a single adult sheep cell and a process of nuclear transfer
questions.correctAnswer: Using a single adult sheep cell and a process of nuclear transfer

questions.question: How long did Dolly the sheep live?
According to the text, what is the current status of human cloning?
options: It is perfected and ready for use
It is still not perfect and certainly not ready for human application
It has been successfully performed on humans
questions.correctAnswer: It is still not perfect and certainly not ready for human application

questions.question: What time period did Ian Wilmot live in?
What time period did Keith Campbell live in?
Aristotle was an ancient Greek philosopher who lived during which period?
Leeuwenhoek invented a method for making strong lenses that could magnify up to how many times?
Who is regarded as the father of genetics according to the review questions in the unit?
